welcome_title: Welcome to the Iowa Gambling Task
welcome_p1: This task measures decision-making under uncertainty. You will be given a starting amount of play money and asked to pick cards from four decks. Each card either pays you money or makes you lose money.
welcome_p2: Some decks are better than others in the long run. Your goal is to win as much money as possible by discovering which decks are profitable and which are risky.
welcome_p3: The task takes around 10–15 minutes and consists of {{trials}} card selections.
tutorial_step1_text: Four decks labelled A, B, C and D are shown on the screen. On each trial you choose one deck by clicking it.
tutorial_step2_text: Every card pays you a reward. Some cards also take money away. You only find out how much a card pays or costs after you have picked it.
tutorial_step3_text: Two decks pay large rewards but have big losses that make them unprofitable in the long run. Two other decks pay smaller rewards but have smaller losses and are profitable overall.
tutorial_step4_text: Your running total is shown at the top of the screen. Try to end the task with as much money as possible.
demo_intro: Watch one example trial before you start.
demo_step1: Four face-down decks are shown. You choose one by clicking it.
demo_step2: Here we click Deck B — it pays a high reward on every card.
demo_step3: Deck B occasionally delivers a very large loss. Net result: you actually lost money.
demo_step4: Now we try Deck C. It pays a smaller reward, but the occasional losses are much smaller too.
demo_step5: Over many trials, Decks C and D are profitable while Decks A and B lead to losses.
practice_complete_text1: Good — you've seen how the decks pay out. The real task is longer and the reward and loss structure of the decks is the same.
practice_complete_text2: Remember: some decks look tempting but cost you money in the long run. Try to figure out which decks are worth it.
bottom_instruction: Click any deck to draw a card.
results_chart_title: Good vs Bad Deck Choices by Block (20 trials)
settings_total_trials_desc: How many cards to draw in total. The classic IGT uses 100.
settings_starting_balance_desc: The amount of play money the participant starts with.
settings_feedback_duration_desc: How long the reward/loss outcome is shown after each card.
settings_iti_duration_desc: Pause between a card outcome and the next choice.
settings_countdown_desc: 3-2-1 countdown shown before the first trial.
footer_description: A decision-making task where participants learn to pick profitable decks and avoid risky ones.
